senses.definition: in many different locations or scattered over an area without a specific pattern
used to mean that something can no longer be seen

examples.x: After the magician's trick, the rabbit disappeared out of sight.
The ship disappeared out of sight as it sailed away from the harbor.
The bird flew out of sight into the distance.
The car turned the corner and was soon out of sight.

senses.definition: near a particular location or object

examples.x: The grocery store is close by, so we can walk there in just a few minutes.
There's a café close by where we can grab a quick lunch.
The park is close by, so we can go for a walk there.
I live close by, so I can be there in a few minutes.
There's a pharmacy close by where we can pick up the prescription.

senses.definition: in a direction or location that is beyond the established limits

examples.x: He hit the ball out of bounds.
The hikers wandered out of bounds and got lost in the wilderness.
The golf ball landed out of bounds, beyond the marked area of play.
The car drove out of bounds and onto the private property.
The drone flew out of bounds and into restricted airspace.

senses.definition: used to convey that something has occurred or happened very recently or narrowly

examples.x: She only just finished her presentation before the deadline.
He only just avoided getting hit by the car as it swerved past him.
We only just found out about the meeting; otherwise, we would have missed it.
We only just made it to the airport before the flight departed.
They only just managed to catch the last train home.

senses.definition: used to indicate a partial or limited level of something

examples.x: She agreed with him to some degree, but had reservations about certain aspects.
The plan succeeded to some extent, but not entirely as expected.
His understanding of the subject is accurate to some degree, but there are gaps in his knowledge.
I enjoyed the movie to some extent, although it wasn't my favorite.
The project met the deadline to some degree, but there were delays along the way.
She trusts him to some extent, but there are moments of doubt.

senses.definition: used to specify the degree or extent to which one thing is influenced or determined by another thing or condition

examples.x: The success of the project was influenced to the extent that the team's collaboration and dedication played a crucial role.
His health deteriorated to the extent that he could no longer perform his daily activities.
She succeeded in her career to the extent that she was willing to work hard and persevere.
The company's profitability increased to the extent that it invested in new technology and expanded its market reach.
The outcome of the negotiations hinged on compromise, to the extent that each party was willing to give up something.

senses.definition: used to indicate that something is true or applicable only to some extent or degree

examples.x: The rainy weather contributed to the flooding in part, but the main cause was the clogged drainage system.
The project's success was due in part to the team's collaboration.
Her decision to resign was motivated in part by dissatisfaction with her work environment.
The company's profits declined in part because of changes in market trends.
The delay in the construction was caused in part by adverse weather conditions.

senses.definition: indicating that something has had a partial impact or influence

examples.x: The team's efforts, in some measure, contributed to the success of the project, but external factors also played a role.
His success can be attributed to his hard work, intelligence, and luck in some measure.
The improvement in the company's performance can be attributed to changes in management in some measure.
The decline in biodiversity is due to human activity in some measure.
The increase in crime rates can be attributed to socioeconomic factors in some measure.

senses.definition: in a particular manner or fashion, often used to describe a specific aspect or perspective

examples.x: He was a mentor to me in a way, always offering guidance and support when I needed it.
She acted as a second mother to me in a way, always there to listen and offer advice.
The mountain became a symbol of strength to me in a way, reminding me of the challenges I had overcome.
She's like a sister to me in a way, always there to listen and support me.

senses.definition: used to indicate the degree or extent to which something has reached a particular level or condition

examples.x: His dedication to the task was to a point that he worked late into the night to ensure its completion.
The temperature rose to a point that it became unbearable to be outside.
The tension between them escalated to a point that they could no longer communicate effectively.
The company expanded its operations to a point that it outgrew its current infrastructure.
The river flooded to a point that it submerged nearby homes and roads.

senses.definition: staying within the bounds of rationality
